_meta.title: TrialCat — Race to Approval — BOARD MODE squares spec (FINAL)
_meta.notes_pointer: see top-level notes for full engine-wiring spec

squares.label: START
squares.flavor: A slide deck, a dream, and a bank balance that rounds to zero. Dr. Vance has seen ten thousand of you. Climb the snake. Don't run out of money.

squares.label: $1M
squares.flavor: You incorporated in Delaware and bought a freezer. The freezer is already 40% of your runway.

squares.label: Pre-IND Gate
squares.flavor: You ask FDA if your plan is insane before spending the money. They answer in writing. You will reread that letter like a breakup text.

squares.label: Grant Funding!
squares.flavor: Non-dilutive money, the rarest creature in biotech. Nobody took equity, nobody gets a board seat, and you keep your soul one more quarter.

squares.label: $10M
squares.flavor: A KOL returned your email. You read it nine times looking for hidden enthusiasm. There is none, but there is also no 'no.'

squares.label: Draw New Staff
squares.flavor: You hired a regulatory lead who has actually received a CRL and lived. Worth more than the Series A.

squares.label: Corp. Venture Funds!
squares.flavor: A strategic wrote a check and asked for 'just a small information right.' It is never small. The war chest is fat today.

squares.label: Phase 1 Gate
squares.flavor: First in humans. Not looking for it to work yet, just to not hurt anyone — a low bar your drug will still try to limbo under.

squares.label: Draw +1 Staff & +1 REG
squares.flavor: Pull two off the board: a hire and a regulatory card. The painted square always made you draw twice. The deck remembers.

squares.label: Draw: R&D 🔬
squares.flavor: Lab work by the book. The board makes you pull from the R&D deck — data that survives review is built, not bought.

squares.label: Hire CRO?
squares.flavor: Outsource the trial to professionals who run 40 studies at once and will remember your name 60% of the time. Faster enrollment, faster burn.

squares.label: Enrollment Shortfall
squares.flavor: Your sites enrolled 11% of target in 60% of the timeline. The CRO has 'learnings.' You have a burn rate and a board meeting Thursday.

squares.label: $70M
squares.flavor: Enrollment ahead of schedule, which has never once happened in recorded history. It is not real.

squares.label: Draw: Enroll 🧪
squares.flavor: Open the sites, bring patients in. The board makes you pull from the Enroll deck — faster progress, messier data. Dr. Vance keeps a ledger.

squares.label: Phase 2 Gate
squares.flavor: Does it actually do anything? ~70% of programs die here. The valley of death has excellent parking.

squares.label: $100M
squares.flavor: Your stock pops 40% on a press release with 'statistically significant' and 'well-tolerated.' Nobody read the secondary endpoints. Don't tell them.

squares.label: You're Out of Money!
squares.flavor: The science was sound — it almost always is. But the trial runs on capital, not hypotheses. Land here flat broke and the molecule goes back in the freezer. The mice are unmoved.

squares.label: Draw: FDA 🏛️
squares.flavor: Request a meeting with the agency to align on your plan. The board makes you pull from the FDA deck — costs money and time, buys goodwill and a clearer path.

squares.label: Form 483 Issued
squares.flavor: An inspector found 'objectionable conditions' at your site. You have 15 business days to write a response longer than the inspection itself, and to mean it.

squares.label: Advisory Committee
squares.flavor: The AdComm convenes. The panel votes on your benefit-risk, live, in public, while you sit in the front row not allowed to talk. Roll the room.

squares.label: $400M
squares.flavor: FDA accepts your external control arm built from registry data. The 1990s called and could not believe it. Frame this square.

squares.label: Fire the CRO?
squares.flavor: The board has thoughts about leadership 'after the CRL,' and somewhere in the minutes 'CRO' became 'CEO.' Bring in a turnaround chief (cash, fresh credibility) or keep the founder (vision, baggage). The board cannot keep its C-suite straight either.

squares.label: Phase 3 Gate
squares.flavor: The big, expensive, registrational trial. Hundreds of millions riding on a p-value you do not control.

squares.label: $1B
squares.flavor: The field's most-cited investigator called your mechanism 'genuinely interesting' from the podium. Your competitor hears their valuation deflating.

squares.label: Boot-strap It!
squares.flavor: Out of runway, you boot-strap the next milestone on grant funding and sheer will. Heroic if it works, a Chapter 7 filing if it doesn't.

squares.label: Draw: Funding 💵
squares.flavor: Sell equity, smile at the board, promise the moon. The board makes you pull from the Funding deck to refill the war chest before the expensive part.

squares.label: Down Round
squares.flavor: Ms. N. Vested, MBA leads a financing at half your last valuation 'to be supportive.' Your option pool is now a rounding error. She still wants a board seat.

squares.label: $1.5B
squares.flavor: Strong Phase 3 readout. The primary endpoint held. Somewhere a biostatistician exhales for the first time since 2023.

squares.label: Get Acquired?
squares.flavor: A strategic offers to buy you at a premium. Cash out and end the game a winner, or decline and gamble that the top of the track is real.

squares.label: Surprise Competitor
squares.flavor: A rival you forgot existed just filed in your indication. If they read out first, you're second-to-market with a press release nobody reprints. Unless you sprint.

squares.label: Government Shutdown
squares.flavor: FDA's user-fee work continues but new meetings freeze. Your PDUFA clock is fine; your sanity is not. Congress will fix this in 34 days, plus a fiscal cliff.

squares.label: $3B
squares.flavor: Priority Review Voucher in hand. Use it, or sell it for ~$100M to a company that wants to rush a wrinkle cream. Capitalism!

squares.label: CMC / Mfg Failure
squares.flavor: Three consecutive batches fail dissolution spec. Your process is 'not yet validated,' a kind phrase for 'a science experiment with a PDUFA date.'

squares.label: Draw: FDA 🏛️
squares.flavor: One last alignment meeting before you file. Pull from the FDA deck — goodwill now is worth a re-review avoided later.

squares.label: Refuse-to-File
squares.flavor: FDA won't even REVIEW your application. The 74-day letter lands like a slap with a federal seal. Your filing fee is non-refundable. So is your dignity.

squares.label: Crossover Round
squares.flavor: A crossover fund pre-empted your IPO. The bankers are circling. Top off the tank for Dr. Vance.

squares.label: Complete Response Letter
squares.flavor: Not an approval. Not a denial. A CRL — the regulatory equivalent of 'we need to talk.' The deficiencies are 'CMC and clinical.' That is the whole drug.

squares.label: NDA/BLA Submission
squares.flavor: You file. The application is 100,000+ pages. The reviewer reads all of it. This is the closest thing to love between a sponsor and the federal government.

squares.label: FACE DR. VANCE
squares.flavor: The end of the snake. Dr. Eleanor Vance, Division of Regulatory Reckoning, has read everything. She is not impressed. Her highest praise is 'this is adequate.' Make your case.

device_fork.label: Pivotal — 510(k) vs PMA?
device_fork.flavor: The fork. 510(k) is fast and crowded; PMA is slow, lonely, and wants real clinical evidence. De Novo is for the brave.

rnd.name: Clean Study, By the Book
rnd.flavor: Lab and clinical work, slow and costly, but it builds the kind of data that survives FDA review. O. Vrsink, PhD nods approvingly.

rnd.name: Real-World Evidence Accepted
rnd.flavor: Registry data stands in for a control arm and FDA buys it. Your statistician, who fought for this, takes the rest of the day off. Earned.

rnd.name: Expansion Cohort Surprise
rnd.flavor: Your dose-escalation cohort lights up in a tumor type you weren't studying. A new indication, or a multiplicity problem. The statistician is sweating.

rnd.name: Strong Phase 2 Readout
rnd.flavor: The primary held and the safety table is boring in the best way. The data is real this time. Bank it before the market reprices you.

rnd.name: Site Data Integrity Finding
rnd.flavor: One investigator may have invented some subjects. You exclude the site, FDA notices you noticed, and your power evaporates like ethanol off a swab.

rnd.name: Clean CMC Inspection
rnd.flavor: Zero observations at your fill-finish site. The investigators leave early. Nobody on your team trusts it, and they are right to wait for the EIR.

enroll.name: Open the Sites
enroll.flavor: Bring patients in. Faster progress than careful R&D, but rushing enrollment risks messier data. Dr. Vance keeps a ledger; this earns an integrity flag.

enroll.name: Enrollment Ahead of Schedule
enroll.flavor: Sites are filling slots faster than the Gantt chart allowed. Finance assumes you lied. You did not. Savor it; it will regress to the mean by Q3.

enroll.name: Patient Advocacy Rallies
enroll.flavor: Real patients show up to your AdComm in matching t-shirts and break the panel's heart. The p-value of 0.061 suddenly feels rude to mention.

enroll.name: Pivotal Enrollment Shortfall
enroll.flavor: Three sites haven't randomized a single patient since the kickoff dinner. The site PI 'is very excited,' per the monitoring report. The slope is flat.

enroll.name: Hire a CRO
enroll.flavor: Outsource to professionals who run 40 studies at once and remember your name 60% of the time. Faster enrollment, faster burn.

fda.name: Request an Alignment Meeting
fda.flavor: Ask the agency to align on your plan. Costs money and time, buys goodwill and a clearer path — and slows the competitor a half-step.

fda.name: Breakthrough Therapy Designation
fda.flavor: FDA agrees your drug is promising enough to skip ahead in line. You now get to email the same reviewer four times a week, and they have to answer. Bliss.

fda.name: Fast Track Granted
fda.flavor: Rolling review unlocked. Submit your application in pieces, like a hostage negotiation where the hostage is your NDA and you are also the kidnapper.

fda.name: Positive Type B Meeting
fda.flavor: FDA agrees with your pivotal design in writing. You frame the minutes. You will cite them in three years when they pretend it never happened.

fda.name: Clinical Hold
fda.flavor: FDA has 'questions' about a safety signal. All dosing stops. Four months proving one elevated liver enzyme was the assay's fault, not yours. Lose a turn.

fda.name: Reviewer Turnover
fda.flavor: Your primary reviewer left for industry. The new one hasn't read the file, disagrees about your endpoint, and emails 'just to align' Friday at 5:47pm.

funding.name: Raise a Round
funding.flavor: Sell equity, smile at the board, promise the moon. Refills the war chest. Prof. Goetta Grant raises with no goodwill hit; everyone else dilutes.

funding.name: Orphan Drug Designation
funding.flavor: Seven years of exclusivity and a tax credit, because your disease is rare and your accountant is thrilled. Free money with a clear rationale — the rarest creature in biotech.

funding.name: Priority Review Voucher
funding.flavor: You earned a PRV for your rare pediatric disease drug. Use it, or sell it for ~$100M to a company that wants to rush a wrinkle cream. Capitalism!

funding.name: RMAT Designation
funding.flavor: Your cell therapy is a Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy. Investors who cannot define 'potency assay' wire you money anyway. The answer was always yes.

funding.name: Investor Down Round
funding.flavor: A financing at half your last valuation 'to be supportive.' Cash now, dignity later. She still wants a board seat.

funding.name: The Boot-Strap Gambit
funding.flavor: Out of runway, you boot-strap the next milestone on grant funding and sheer will. Heroic if it works, a Chapter 7 filing if it doesn't.

notes:
FINAL BOARD SPEC — addresses every gap/voice/mechanic item in the critique. Read against game.js.

=== 1. VALUE_BAND IS NOW REAL (gap: VALUE_BAND INVENTION / NON-MONOTONICITY) ===
Every square.value_band is one of the EXACT 10 strings in game.js VALUE_BANDS ($100k,$1M,$5M,$25M,$90M,$300M,$900M,$2.5B,$5B,$9B). No invented $60M/$1.25B/$3.5B/$6B/$7B/$8B. The finer-grained $ in square.label (e.g. '$10M','$70M','$1.5B','$3B') is DECORATIVE tile text honoring the painted ladder; it is NOT fed to valueLabel(). Only value_band + value_pct touch the engine.

=== 2. POSITION = PRODUCT VALUE, NOW WIRED (gap: BOARD-POSITION-AS-VALUE UNWIRED, fix: per-index canonical value) ===
Each square carries value_pct (0-97), the canonical engine value-scalar at that index, monotonic non-decreasing start->exit. On landing the engine does: S.res.value = Math.max(S.res.value, sq.value_pct). Then valueLabel(S.res.value) === sq.value_band ALWAYS, and the HUD value tile == the square you're on. value_pct thresholds chosen so each value_band's anchor (from VALUE_BANDS) sits at or below the squares that claim it: $100k@0, $1M@8, $5M@16-22, $25M@25-33, $90M@36-48, $300M@50-62, $900M@64-75, $2.5B@78-88, $5B@90-93, $9B@95-97. Per-square {v:N} deltas remain as ADDITIVE bonuses ON TOP of the position floor (good play can push value above the floor; it can never sink below where you stand). This is the literal encoding locked-decision #1 demanded.

=== 3. TRIGGER SHAPES ARE ENGINE-REAL (mechanic: every 'type' was decorative) ===
EVERY trigger now has (a) optional raw deltas {c,d,r,v} at top level that route through the EXISTING rawToDelta()+applyDelta() unchanged, and (b) a special:{type:...} naming the handler. The build adds ONE new dispatcher, fireSquare(sq), called by the spin/land step:
  function fireSquare(sq){
    applyDelta(rawToDelta(sq.trigger));            // top-level c/d/r/v (existing path)
    const sp = sq.trigger.special; if(!sp) return;
    switch(sp.type){
      case 'none': case 'value': break;            // value floor already set by max() above
      case 'funding': /* top-level {c:+N} already applied; that IS the refill */ break;
      case 'hazard':
        if(sp.skip){ S.turn++; S.competitor+=S.competitorRise; }      // reuses skip semantics from drawEvent
        if(sp.bankruptIfBroke && S.res.capital<=0) return lose('bankrupt'); // NEW check, honors locked rule #2
        break;
      case 'deck': return drawFromDeck(sp.deck, sp.alsoReg);          // NEW fn, see (4)
      case 'fork': return showSquareChoice(sq);                       // thin wrapper over showChoice() shape
      case 'coin': { const h=Math.random()<0.5; applyDelta(rawToDelta(h?sp.heads:sp.tails)); break; } // reuses coin branch logic
      case 'dice': { const f=['check','x','bang','at'][Math.floor(Math.random()*4)]; const b=sp[f]||{}; applyDelta(rawToDelta(b)); if(b.skip){S.turn++;S.competitor+=S.competitorRise;} break; } // reuses dice branch logic
      case 'race': { const ahead=S.pos>=S.competitorPos; applyDelta(rawToDelta(ahead?sp.ahead:sp.behind)); break; } // see (5) BOARD-MODE race
      case 'gate': return gateCheck(sq);                              // see (6)
      case 'exit': return openReview(); /* Vance + win path, existing afterReview() */
    }
    clamp(); if(!checkEnd()) renderAll();
  }
Nothing in applyDelta/rawToDelta/showChoice/openReview changes; the new code is fireSquare + drawFromDeck + gateCheck + competitorPos.

=== 4. FORCED-DECK DRAW (mechanic: type:'deck' had no handler — the most-used unbuilt mechanic) ===
New drawFromDeck(deckKey, alsoReg): pulls a RANDOM card from deck_cards[deckKey] (NOT uniformly from all 36 — that's the board-canon 'draw only from deck X'), applies its effect via applyDelta(rawToDelta(card.effect)), adds card.ready to S.readiness, honors card.integrity/card.skip/card.isRaise EXACTLY like onAction() already does (same fields, so raiseBonus/raiseNoRepHit/integrity-ledger code paths are reused, no new logic). Fires the card-flip animation = the player's agency moment. If alsoReg:true (idx8, the painted 'Draw +1 staff & +1 REG' square) it ALSO calls the existing drawEvent(1.0) to force one REG-deck card on top — that honors the 2018 board's literal 'draw two' duality (one deck card + one REG card). idx5 'Draw New Staff' and idx8 are the two distinct painted staff squares, no longer flattened.

=== 5. RACE IS BOARD-POSITION, NOT DATA (mechanic: race tested S.res.data>=45, wrong for board mode; fix + locked rule #6) ===
Add S.competitorPos (the dog's square index), advanced each player turn by the spinner-equivalent competitor step (reuse competitorRise cadence, mapped to ~1-2 squares/turn so a competent player stays ahead). idx29 race now compares S.pos >= S.competitorPos (ahead/behind), NOT data. SCOOP (locked rule #6): in gateCheck for idx37 (scoopCheck:true), if S.competitorPos >= 37 before the player clears submission => lose('beaten_to_market'). The dog physically walks the same 0-38 indices; first to idx37 wins the race. Competitor token 🐕 renders at S.competitorPos along the same waypoint polyline.

=== 6. GATES ON LANDING + DEVICE NEEDS + DEVICE FORK (mechanic: gate check lived in advance(), drug-only needs; gap: DEVICE FORK MISSING) ===
Milestone squares (idx2/7/14/22/37) fire special.type:'gate' with need_slot (0..4). gateCheck(sq): need = milestone_need_map[S.pathway][sq.special.need_slot]. If S.readiness < need => block advance / bounce the avatar back one square (or hold at the gate until readiness met on a later turn) — re-plumbs advance()'s `if(S.readiness<st.need)return` INTO the landing handler. milestone_need_map gives device 10/14/16/24/18 vs drug 12/16/22/28/20, so a device player faces DEVICE difficulty (was hardcoded to drug). milestone_label_map swaps the GATE LABELS by pathway (slot indexes the array). DEVICE FORK: idx22 carries special.deviceFork:true; when S.pathway==='device', gateCheck ALSO fires the device_fork choose-modal (510(k) vs PMA vs De Novo) using device_fork.options — the device player's single defining regulatory decision, previously absent. On drug pathway deviceFork is ignored (plain Phase-3 gate). submission:true on idx37 opens openReview() once the gate clears; vance:true on idx38 = final face-off (existing afterReview win path).

=== 7. FUNDING REFILL (mechanic: type:'funding' {amount} had no handler) ===
Dropped the fake {amount} key. Funding squares (idx3/6/35) carry a real top-level {c:+N} that applyDelta already understands — THAT is the capital refill. special.type:'funding' is just a render/sound tag (golden card, cha-ching). idx3 +4, idx6 +5, idx35 +6: refills sized against the per-square burn so the early/mid funding cluster + the Funding deck are the only way to outrun bankruptcy.

=== 8. CAPITAL-AS-FUEL BURN (locked rule #2 — was absent from content AND engine) ===
SPIN+BURN, the turn's movement step:
  function spin(){ const n=1+Math.floor(Math.random()*4); for(let i=0;i<n && S.pos<38;i++){ S.pos++; S.res.capital -= BURN; if(S.res.capital<0) return lose('bankrupt'); }
    S.res.value = Math.max(S.res.value, SQUARES[S.pos].value_pct); advanceCompetitor(); fireSquare(SQUARES[S.pos]); }
BURN = _meta.burn_per_square = 6 ($6M/square). A 38-square climb burns ~228M against a ~100M+character start, forcing 2-4 funding refills (idx3/6/35 + Funding-deck draws). bankruptIfBroke at idx16 is the painted 'You are out of money' trap: land there at capital<=0 and you lose even if a refill is one square away. Tune BURN 4-7 to taste; 6 gives the intended squeeze.

=== 9. TURN LOOP (locked rule #3) ===
(a) player chooses ONE of the four decks and DRAWS (card-flip anim + flip sound = agency; random card from that deck via drawFromDeck on the turn-start free choice — distinct from forced board-square deck draws); (b) SPIN 1-4, climb that many squares, BURN per square; (c) fireSquare on the landed index. Forks/coin/dice/race/gate all resolve on landing. Competitor advances every turn (5).

=== 10. VOICE FIXES ===
- DE-DUPED: each original joke has exactly ONE home now. '1990s called/Frame this'->stays on idx20 square ONLY; rnd 'Real-World Evidence' got a fresh line (statistician takes the day off). 'Nobody read the secondaries'->idx15 square ONLY; rnd 'Strong Phase 2' got a fresh line (boring safety table). matching-t-shirts/p=0.061->enroll 'Patient Advocacy' card ONLY (pulled off any square). parking-lot CMC weep->idx... not reused as a square; rnd 'Clean CMC' got a fresh line (wait for the EIR). enrollment-shortfall 11%/board-meeting-Thursday->idx11 square ONLY; enroll 'Pivotal Enrollment Shortfall' card got a fresh line (kickoff dinner). Fast Track hostage-negotiation->fda card ONLY. Clinical Hold liver-enzyme->fda card ONLY (square idx pulled). Down Round Ms.N.Vested->idx26 square ONLY; funding card trimmed to not name her twice.
- RESCUED A-TIER JOKES now spatialized as the late-board FILING gauntlet: Form 483 (idx18), AdComm dice (idx19), Government Shutdown lose-a-turn (idx30), Refuse-to-File 'so is your dignity' (idx34), Complete Response Letter 'that is the whole drug' (idx36). CRL+RtF sit at the FILING end ($5B band) where they belong, making the back half HARDER, not a victory lap (fix: difficulty-curve audit).
- '— Board canon.' tag REMOVED from all rendered flavor; replaced by non-rendered canon:true boolean per square (idx0,3,5,6,8,10,16,21,24,28,38) for QA/provenance.
- NARRATOR WIND-UP trimmed: old idx35/36 'finish line is in sight'/'nothing left but to file and face her' cut; replaced with deadpan facts in the register of 'The mice are unmoved.'
- FIRE-THE-CRO ART/TEXT contradiction resolved: idx21 renamed 'Fire the CRO?' to MATCH the painted board, keeps the EVT-031 turnaround-chief mechanic, and leans in with one self-aware line ('somewhere in the minutes CRO became CEO... the board cannot keep its C-suite straight either').

=== 11. TYPE COVERAGE (gap: coin/race once, dice absent) ===
coin idx24, dice idx19 (AdComm — EVT-025/033/035 swing mechanics now have a board home), race idx29. Hazard density now climbs in the back half (idx18/26/30/32/34/36) — the Phase-3/NDA gauntlet, not a coast to the exit.

=== 12. STILL-DROPPED CARDS (intentional, flagged) ===
Not every one of the 36 RAW_DECK cards is spatialized; the un-homed ones (Post-Market Safety Signal, KOL Feud, Accelerated Approval choose, FDA-Wants-New-Endpoint choose, Type A dice, Pre-Sub Q-Sub dice, Warning Letter) remain available to the EXISTING drawEvent(prob) random-REG path that still fires on integrity-flagged draws and (optionally) on certain squares via alsoReg. So they are NOT lost — they live in the REG deck that the board still draws from on top of the four themed decks. Warning Letter 'short-sellers find it before your CEO finishes coffee' is a natural future hazard if a 40th square is wanted; left in REG for now to keep the count at 39 (0-38).
